name: Monte Naranco Circular Trail
name_original: Iglesia de San Pedro y Cristo del Naranco — circular
description: Hike the 7.1-mile (11.4 km) Iglesia de San Pedro y Cristo del Naranco circular route for moderate climbs, lush forests, and panoramic views
region_intro: Hiking around Oviède offers access to a diverse landscape characterized by green mountains, dense forests, and powerful rivers. The region features varied terrain, from the elevated paths of Monte Naranco to the serene river routes. Hikers can explore areas with significant elevation changes and trails that follow ancient pilgrimage paths. Best hiking trails around Oviède The most popular hiking route is San Miguel de Lillo y Santa María del Naranco — circular, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends Monte Naranco, passing significant pre-Romanesque churches. Another top favourite among local hikers is Iglesia de San Pedro y Cristo del Naranco — circular, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This trail also explores Monte Naranco, offering views of the Cristo del Naranco monument. Local hikers also love the Oviedo to Ermita del Carmen via the Camino Primitivo — circular, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail leading through sections of the historic Camino Primitivo, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Oviède is defined by green mountains, dense forests, and river paths, including sections of the Camino Primitivo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate ascents to gentler riverside walks. The routes in Oviède are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Oviède's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking experiences can I expect around Oviède?
A: Hiking around Oviède offers a diverse landscape of green mountains, dense forests, and powerful rivers. You'll find varied terrain, from the elevated paths of Monte Naranco with significant elevation changes to gentler riverside walks along the Nora River. The region also features sections of the historic Camino Primitivo, providing a mix of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Are there many hiking trails available in the Oviède area?
A: Yes, the Oviède area boasts a wide selection of routes. There are over 190 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. Approximately 68 are considered easy, 111 are moderate, and 17 are more difficult, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails near Oviède?
A: Absolutely. For families, the Path of the River Nora is an excellent choice. It's a 9 km tarmac path that follows the serene Nora River, ideal for walking, cycling, or pushchairs. Another great option is the Route of the Caldas, a 6 km circular, asphalted river path perfect for birdwatching and leisurely strolls with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Oviède?
A: Many trails in the Oviède region are dog-friendly, though it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in nature reserves or areas with livestock. The Path of the River Nora and the Finnish Trail on Monte Naranco are popular choices for walks with dogs, offering open spaces and pleasant scenery.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Oviède?
A: Yes, Oviède offers several popular circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353101848/san-miguel-de-lillo-y-santa-maria-del-naranco-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel de Lillo y Santa María del Naranco — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.2 km trail that ascends Monte Naranco. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048068600/oviedo-to-ermita-del-carmen-via-the-camino-primitivo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oviedo to Ermita del Carmen via the Camino Primitivo — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.2 km path exploring sections of the historic pilgrimage route.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while hiking?
A: The trails around Oviède are rich with points of interest. On Monte Naranco, you can visit the pre-Romanesque churches of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1070132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel de Lillo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1070128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Mary of Naranco&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Heart of Jesus Monument&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5774152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guanga Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; route is particularly scenic, and the famous Senda del Oso (Bear Trail) offers a chance to see bears in a semi-wild environment.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5774152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guanga Waterfalls Route&lt;/a&gt; is a less than 5 km trail that leads to a picturesque waterfall amidst a unique natural setting. It's especially beautiful in spring when the water flow is strong.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Oviède?
A: The best time for hiking in Oviède is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the landscapes are vibrant with either blooming flowers or rich autumnal colors. Summers can be warm, while winters are generally mild but can bring rain.

Q: How can I access hiking trails around Oviède using public transport?
A: Many trails, especially those closer to the city like Monte Naranco, are accessible by local bus services from Oviedo. For routes further afield, such as the Senda del Oso, public transport options might be more limited, and it's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider carpooling. For general information on getting around Oviedo, you can visit the official tourism website: &lt;a href="https://www.visitoviedo.info/en_GB/que-hacer/naturaleza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitoviedo.info&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Oviède?
A: The hiking routes in Oviède are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's green mountains, dense forests, and well-maintained river paths, highlighting the variety of terrain and the historical significance of routes like the Camino Primitivo.

Q: Are there any challenging day hikes near Oviède?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the Oviède area offers more difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353104356/from-la-manjoya-to-the-senda-verde-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From La Manjoya to the Senda Verde — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.9 km hike with significant elevation changes, providing a rewarding experience for experienced hikers.

Q: Where can I find parking for popular hiking spots like Monte Naranco?
A: Parking is generally available at the base of Monte Naranco, near the access points to trails leading up the mountain. Specific parking areas can be found near the pre-Romanesque monuments or at designated trailheads. For other popular routes like the Path of the River Nora or the Senda del Oso, dedicated parking facilities are often provided at the start of the trails.
tips: The arch of the church of San Isidoro is one of the most striking pieces hidden in Campo San Francisco.
Another beautiful church in Oviedo, located above the central station in a small park.

From this exposed location the statue of Christ looks out over Oviedo. He is shown with outstretched arms, but not as the crucified one: they are the blessing hands and outstretched arms of the lovingly drawing Christ. The builders of this monument expressed their desire for God's blessing for their city - and especially its residents.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing the Iglesia de San Pedro y Cristo del Naranco circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike, covering approximately 11.4 kilometers, typically takes around 3 hours and 30 minutes to complete. This duration accounts for the moderate difficulty and elevation changes along the route.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as moderate. While many paths are easily accessible, hikers should be prepared for some steep ascents, particularly when heading towards the Sacred Heart of Jesus Monument (Monte Naranco) and Pico Paisano. It requires good physical fitness.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is easily accessible, with options to reach the starting point by public transport. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the area around Monte Naranco offers various access points. Consider using public transport to avoid potential parking challenges, especially during peak times.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in historical and natural attractions. You'll pass by the Arch of the Old San Isidoro Church and the Church of San Pedro de los Arcos. A major highlight is the Sacred Heart of Jesus Monument (Monte Naranco), offering spectacular panoramic views of Oviedo. Additionally, you can discover the UNESCO World Heritage sites of Santa María del Naranco and San Miguel de Lillo, the Fuente de los Pastores, and the Lavadero de Entrerregueros.

faqs.question: Is the Iglesia de San Pedro y Cristo del Naranco circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the natural spaces on Monte Naranco are generally enjoyed by walkers, including those with dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas that might be shared with cyclists or near historical sites, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no specific permit required, nor are there any entrance fees to hike this circular trail or access the natural areas of Monte Naranco. The historical monuments along the route may have separate visiting hours or fees, but the trail itself is freely accessible.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon start is advisable. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially wet or icy conditions on some sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, ranging from easily accessible paths to sections with steep uphill climbs and potentially loose stones. You'll experience lush forest scenery, especially on the ascent of Monte Naranco. Be aware that some parts of the trail can be shared with downhill mountain bike routes.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! As you gain elevation, you'll be treated to continuous panoramic views of Oviedo. The most iconic viewpoint is from the Sacred Heart of Jesus Monument (Monte Naranco), which offers some of the most spectacular vistas. Pico Paisano is another significant point for excellent views.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other known routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ruta Xixon - Puertu Payares, Pista Finlandesa, and Avenida de los Monumentos, among others, which contribute to the diverse experience of the hike.

faqs.question: Are there any tips for a more serene hiking experience?
faqs.answer: To enjoy a more serene experience and better light for photography, consider hiking during weekdays or in the early morning or late afternoon. This helps avoid larger crowds and offers beautiful lighting conditions for the panoramic views.
